sample.title: What Brexit Cost the World
sample.description:
In June 2016, the UK voted to leave the European Union. Ten years later, Bloomberg Originals unpacks the economic cost of Brexit and the political forces it unleashed.

The Brexit vote sparked a surge in populism and triggered years of parliamentary deadlock that crippled a series of prime ministers. We examine the factors that drove the Leave vote, and how it changed politics both in the UK and around the world. We also use Bloomberg Economics modeling to analyze its impact on the UK economy. The conventional wisdom has always been that Britain’s exit would be a financial calamity. We do the math to see whether that’s true.

Far from settling the issue for a generation, Brexit continues to loom large over British politics, with the matter of Britain’s relationship with the EU still high on the domestic agenda. Meanwhile, with the US under Donald Trump retreating from old alliances, Britain and its European allies are confronting their most challenging geopolitical moment since the end of the Cold War.

Indeed, in the years since leaving the EU, successive UK prime ministers have sought closer alignment with Brussels. Some recent polling even suggests a growing majority of the British public would now vote to rejoin the bloc. Any attempts to do so would be met by fierce resistance, however, with one of the foremost architects of Brexit, Reform UK leader Nigel Farage, now a favorite to become the country’s next prime minister.

Explore these and other related stories in more depth on Bloomberg.com:

The Day That Changed the World
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2026-05-29/how-britain-europe-and-the-west-were-changed-by-brexit

What Reversing Brexit Would Mean for the UK Economy
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2026-06-09/what-rolling-back-brexit-vote-would-mean-for-the-uk-economy

00:00: Introduction
02:38: Title Card
02:43: The Vote

sample.title: What The U.S.-Iran Deal Could Mean For Markets And Your Wallet
sample.description:
President Trump and Iranian leaders signed a memorandum of understanding that should reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lower oil prices and ease shipping costs. The deal could bring relief for consumers through cheaper gas, airfare and goods, but risks around regional security, inflation, Fed policy and interest rates remain.

sample.title: How dating became a financial luxury
sample.description:
Half of single Americans say they are going on fewer dates or choosing less expensive activities because of rising costs, according to BMO’s 2026 survey.

But, the cost of dating itself is only part of the story. For millions of users, finding a date means paying for the apps.

sample.title: Where Markets Think Fed Chair Warsh Is Taking Interest Rates
sample.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is floating major revisions to the central bank’s management of assets, inflation and public communications. Markets are pricing in higher odds for future rate hikes, rather than rate cuts as emphasized by Chair Warsh in confirmation hearings. CNBC’s Matt Peterson, Steve Liesman and Jeff Cox report.

Reporters: Steve Liesman, Jeff Cox and Matt Peterson

sample.title: OPEC chief dismisses IEA supply glut forecast as 'critical' Strait of Hormuz reopens
sample.description:
OPEC chief Haitham al-Ghais sat down for an exclusive interview with CNBC on Wednesday.

He dismissed the IEA’s claim of a supply glut brewing in oil markets for 2027.

“What does the IEA see that OPEC and the rest don’t see?” he added.

sample.title: Racers chase fun during Taiwan's Dragon Boat Festival
sample.description:
Dragon boat teams competed in New Taipei as crowds gathered to mark the Dragon Boat Festival, an annual celebration that blends tradition with sport. Team captain Cheng Ai-Hwa said dragon boat paddling requires coordination and collective strength.

sample.title: Top tips for keeping kids safe in the heat. #Summer #HeatHealth #BBCNews
What we know about Bedford train crash. #Bedford #England #Trains #Railway #BBCNews
Drones, dollars and deals - who won the Iran War? | The Security Brief
sample.description:
The Security Brief is on the road - in Switzerland - where the US and Iran were supposed to begin nuclear negotiations over the future of Iran's nuclear programme. 

As the two sides sign the 'Memorandum of Understanding', we compare what the document says to the initial war objectives set by the United States. 

Donald Trump has claimed the US won the war on multiple occasions - but do the facts back that up? This is The Security Brief with Christian Fraser and Mikey Kay. 

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#BBCNews

sample.title: One dead and 11 very seriously injured in Bedford train crash | BBC News
sample.description:
One person has died after two trains collided in Bedford, UK, according to British Transport Police.
 
The East of England Ambulance Service said a total of 89 people have been injured, with 11 of them suffering "very serious injuries".
 
A major incident has been declared, with firefighters, police and ambulance crews at the scene.
 
The trains involved were the 16:40 from Corby to London St Pancras and the 15:50 from Nottingham to London St Pancras.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews
